place: West Freehold CDP, New Jersey, New Jersey

facts.text: The 1‑room West Freehold Schoolhouse on Wemrock Road dates back to the 19th century and once stood on land that was part of the Walker‑Combs‑Hartshorne farm.
Moore’s Tavern and Restaurant, now a local favorite, began life as Mount’s Tavern on the southwest corner of West Freehold’s Main Street.
In 1995, the Freehold Township Committee honored the historic farm that later became Moore’s Inn by designating it a landmark site.
West Freehold sits just two miles from the Battle of Monmouth, and its Village Historic District preserves many colonial‑era structures.
The Burlington Path, a 17th‑century stagecoach route, once ran through West Freehold, linking Burlington and Perth Amboy.
Long before European settlement, the Lenni Lenape Indians were the original inhabitants of the land that became West Freehold.
